books.title: Over and Under the Pond
books.author: Kate Messner
books.rationale: This beautiful picture book takes us on a trip to the pond alongside mother and son. It is a lovely addition to your bookshelf, but scheduling in the lesson plans is flexible for those using their library.

books.title: Bat Loves the Night
books.author: Nicola Davies
books.rationale: This beautiful picture book illuminates the life of a common but less-often-observed creature. It is a lovely addition to your bookshelf, but scheduling in the lesson plans is flexible for those using their library.

books.title: Winter Sleep: A Hibernation Story
books.author: Sean Taylor
books.rationale: This beautiful picture book takes readers on a winter walk with a boy and his grandmother. It is a lovely addition to your bookshelf, but scheduling in the lesson plans is flexible for those using their library.

books.title: A Fruit is a Suitcase for Seeds
books.author: Jean Richards
books.rationale: This beautiful picture book introduces students to the ways that plants use seeds. It is a lovely addition to your bookshelf, but scheduling in the lesson plans is flexible for those using their library.

books.title: Up in the Garden and Down in the Dirt
books.author: Kate Messner
books.rationale: This beautiful picture book takes us on a trip to the garden alongside a girl and her grandmother. It is a lovely addition to your bookshelf, but scheduling in the lesson plans is flexible for those using their library.

books.title: A House Spider's Life
books.author: John Himmelman
books.rationale: This beautiful picture book illuminates the life of a common, but often less appreciated, creature. It is a lovely addition to your bookshelf, but scheduling in the lesson plans is flexible for those using their library.

books.title: Walks with a Naturalist: Living Nature Reader Volume 1
books.author: Melissa Dowland
books.rationale: Our nature reader brings learners alongside an experienced nature educator as she relates her stories with curiosity, delight, and humility.  Volume 1 explores insects, spiders, birds, seeds, and more.

books.title: A Walk in the...
books.author: Rebecca L. Johnson
books.rationale: These beautiful picture books take us on a walk through the biomes, introducing the various plants and animals we might encounter in various seasons. They are a lovely addition to your bookshelf, but scheduling in the lesson plans is flexible for those using their library.

books.title: Monarch and Milkweed
books.author: Helen Frost
books.rationale: This beautiful picture book introduces students to an insect and its relationship with the environment. It is a lovely addition to your bookshelf, but scheduling in the lesson plans is flexible for those using their library.

books.title: Desert Giant
books.author: Barbara Bash
books.rationale: This beautiful picture book introduces students to the saguaro cactus, a keystone species in the desert biome. It is a lovely addition to your bookshelf, but scheduling in the lesson plans is flexible for those using their library.

books.title: Mama Built a Little Nest
books.author: Jennifer Ward
books.rationale: This beautiful picture book introduces students to the variety of ways that birds build homes for their offspring. It is a lovely addition to your bookshelf, but scheduling in the lesson plans is flexible for those using their library.

books.title: The Secret Life of the Skunk
books.author: Laurence Pringle
books.rationale: This beautiful picture book illuminates the life of a common but less-often-observed creature.  It is a lovely addition to your bookshelf, but scheduling in the lesson plans is flexible for those using their library.

books.title: Walks with a Naturalist: Living Nature Reader Volume 2
books.author: Melissa Dowland
books.rationale: Our nature reader brings learners alongside an experienced nature educator as she relates her stories with curiosity, delight, and humility.  Volume 2 explores insects, insect larvae, mammals, some aquatic species, and more.

books.title: Buzztail and Leaper
books.author: Robert M. McClung
books.rationale: Two stories in one, this classic presents two very different animals in the context of their ecosystems. Students read about their habitats, biomes, food chains, life cycles, adaptations, and more.

books.title: Next Time You See a Cloud
books.author: Emily Morgan
books.rationale: This beautiful picture book calls attention to physical features in the larger ecosystem: weather changes and the movement of water. It is a lovely addition to your bookshelf, but scheduling in the lesson plans is flexible for those using their library.

books.title: Going Home
books.author: Marianne Berkes
books.rationale: This lovely picture book introduces students to animal migration as a global phenomenon.  It is a lovely addition to your bookshelf, but scheduling in the lesson plans is flexible for those using their library.

books.title: Christian Liberty Nature Reader Book 4
books.author: Edward J. Shewan
books.rationale: Introduces 21st-century North American students to a variety of common birds, insects, reptiles, and their habits.

books.title: The Universe in You: A Microscopic Journey
books.author: Jason Chin
books.rationale: This stunning picture book introduces students to Things-too-small-to-see.  With beautiful artwork and accessible language, this title is sure to inspire everyone to look more closely.

books.title: To Fly: The Story of the Wright Brothers
books.author: Wendie Old
books.rationale: This book tells the story of an important technology that students have probably observed and maybe even used. Along the way, they learn how the Wright Brothers were driven by curiosity and inspiration and how they persisted to solve one of the biggest challenges of their day.

books.title: The Elephant Scientist
books.author: Caitlin O'Connell and Donna M. Jackson
books.rationale: Challenges arise when wild animals meet human communities. The scientist in this story not only observes elephants, but she also uses what she learns from them, along with what she already knows about sound waves, to collaborate with the local community to navigate such challenges.

books.title: A World in a Drop of Water: Exploring with a Microscope
books.author: Alvin and Virginia Silverstein
books.rationale: This book introduces students to the microscopic creatures that live in natural water. They will examine pond water under a microscope to see the hidden lives of single-celled organisms.

books.title: The Octopus Scientists: Exploring the Mind of a Mollusk
books.author: Sy Montgomery
books.rationale: This book provides students with a closer look at a creature from the family Mollusca. With respect and persistence, the author pursues the mysterious and fascinating octopus - and shares all that she learns with us, of course!

books.title: Life on Surtsey: Iceland's Upstart Island
books.author: Loree Griffin Burns
books.rationale: In 1963, a volcano erupted and created a new island off the coast of Iceland. This book tells the story of the scientists who are watching and recording life unfold through primary succession.

books.title: Science: Grade 4 Lab Book
books.author: Alveary
books.rationale: This document contains all the labs for Grade 4, including instructions, procedures, and leveled guidance for analyses and using a lab notebook. The teacher's primary role is teaching students to follow directions.

books.title: The Hive Detectives: Chronicle of a Honey Bee Catastrophe
books.author: Loree Griffin Burns
books.rationale: This book shows concepts from ecology, mathematics, and chemistry as it invites students into conversation on an important current issue.

books.title: The Tornado Scientist
books.author: Mary Kay Carson
books.rationale: In this captivating story, readers follow a tornado scientist, learn about her life, her work, and her passion to save lives by improving our understanding of the weather.

books.title: Mission to Pluto: The First Visit to an Ice Dwarf and the Kuiper Belt
books.author: Mary Kay Carson
books.rationale: Students learn about the discovery of Pluto and join the scientists who are learning about our solar system. How do we learn about very big things and those that are very far away? We find out on a Mission to Pluto!

books.title: Nature All Around: Plants
books.author: Pamela Hickman
books.rationale: We have chosen this lovely companion to our picture book selection for our 5th graders' introduction to botany. This title uses simple language, introduces new terms, and incorporates artwork that is both beautiful and useful for students who are ready to get to know the world of plants more closely!

books.title: The Forest in the Tree
books.author: Ailsa Wild, Aviva Reed, Briony Barr, & Gregory Crocetti
books.rationale: This lovely picture book brings together important botanical and ecological topics within the context of a story.

books.title: Shanleya's Quest Card Game
books.author: Thomas Elpel
books.rationale: Botany in a Day author, Thomas Elpel, produced this deck of cards to help students begin to notice the patterns that are used to characterize common plant families.

books.title: The Bat Scientists
books.author: Mary Kay Carson
books.rationale: This book provides students with a closer look at an endangered species that might be right in their neighborhood. Follow Dr. Tuttle in his tireless work to understand and protect the bat and to maintain balance in the ecosystem.

books.title: Beetle Busters
books.author: Loree Griffin Burns
books.rationale: The Asian longhorned beetle has devastated forest ecosystems in America. Students are introduced to basic forestry as they learn about the problem of invasive species. The author beautifully invites the reader to consider the challenges of dealing with this complex issue and encourages dialogue and collaboration as part of the problem-solving process.

books.title: Science: Grade 5 Lab Book
books.author: Alveary
books.rationale: This document contains all the labs for Grade 5, including instructions, procedures, and leveled guidance for analyses and using a lab notebook. The teacher's primary role is teaching students to follow directions.

books.title: Plate Tectonics
books.author: Fiona Young-Brown
books.rationale: This text explores the field of plate tectonics from its early history to the current era.  Students will consider how such theories explain the natural phenomena that have contributed to the Earth's development, as well as events such as volcanoes and earthquakes.

books.title: Phineas Gage
books.author: John Fleischman
books.rationale: In this classic story, readers hear the story of a man who suffers an horrific railroad accident.  The resulting brain injury forever changes his life and produces one the most fascinating case studies that neuroscience has ever seen. Note that the author associates social ability and success with human value. Discuss with students as appropriate.

books.title: The Art of Construction
books.author: Mario Salvadori
books.rationale: We love that this book provides hands-on activities for exploring the principles of engineering, allowing students to dip their toes into the engineering process with everyday materials.

books.title: Tracking Trash
books.author: Loree Griffin Burns
books.rationale: Students follow a scientist who uses creative methods to better understand ocean currents and our impact on life within the sea.

books.title: The Wolves and Moose of Isle Royale: Restoring an Island Ecosystem
books.author: Nancy F. Castaldo
books.rationale: Students discover the threatened predator-prey relationship that maintains the Isle Royale ecosystem in this fascinating story. Will scientists be able to restore the balance?  Let's follow this living laboratory experiment.

books.title: The Frog Scientist
books.author: Pamela Turner
books.rationale: Scientists now know that frogs are particularly sensitive to pollution. Readers join the work of scientists who are learning just how chemical run-off affects their life cycle. This text is a particularly good example of the relationship between field and laboratory work and introduces important facets of excellent experimental design "by-the-way."

books.title: Science: Grade 6 Lab Book
books.author: Alveary
books.rationale: This document contains all the labs for Grade 6, including instructions, procedures, and leveled guidance for analyses and using a lab notebook. The teacher's primary role is teaching students to follow directions.

books.title: The Story of Science: Aristotle Leads the Way
books.author: Joy Hakim
books.rationale: This series situates facts and theories within their historical, political, and cultural context.  The author tells the story of (physical) science as a process of developing knowledge that is intimately tied to human society. Ms. Hakim challenges readers to consider differing viewpoints, so as to understand science with uncommon wholeness.

books.title: The Girl Who Drew Butterflies: How Maria Merian's Art Changed Science
books.author: Joyce Sidman
books.rationale: One of the first naturalists to observe live insects directly, Maria Sibylla Merian was also one of the first to document the metamorphosis of the butterfly and the close relationships between insects and plants. In this richly illustrated nonfiction biography, readers meet the "mother" of ecology, a woman who flouted convention in the pursuit of knowledge.

books.title: Science: Grade 7 Lab Book
books.author: Alveary
books.rationale: This document contains all the labs for Grade 7, including instructions, procedures, and leveled guidance for analyses and using a lab notebook. The lab book builds upon those from Form 2 (Grades 4-6) but also supports those new to the program in Form 3 (Grades 7-8). It has been designed for a gentler transition to high school science, as well.

books.title: The Story of Science: Newton At the Center
books.author: Joy Hakim
books.rationale: This series situates facts and theories within their historical, political, and cultural context.  The author tells the story of (physical) science as a process of developing knowledge that is intimately tied to human society. Ms. Hakim challenges readers to consider differing viewpoints, so as to understand science with uncommon wholeness.

books.title: The Story of Science: Einstein Adds a New Dimension
books.author: Joy Hakim
books.rationale: This series situates facts and theories within their historical, political, and cultural context.  The author tells the story of (physical) science as a process of developing knowledge that is intimately tied to human society. Ms. Hakim challenges readers to consider differing viewpoints, so as to understand science with uncommon wholeness.

books.title: Invincible Microbe
books.author: Jim Murphy
books.rationale: Murphy tells the story of an ancient and elusive microbe, weaving together ideas from science, history, and ethics. This author is a master storyteller, and we can't imagine a better way to continue our journey through natural history and into the very timely topic of microbiology and infectious disease.

books.title: Science: Grade 8 Lab Book
books.author: Alveary
books.rationale: This document contains all the labs for Grade 8, including instructions, procedures, and leveled guidance for analyses and using a lab notebook. The lab book is designed to provide a foundation for the high school disciplines.

books.title: Michael Faraday's The Chemical History of a Candle
books.author: Bill Hammack & Don DeCoste
books.rationale: This book introduces modern readers to Michael Faraday’s great nineteenth-century lectures on "The Chemical History of a Candle." Students will be watching the lectures on video, so this text is for teachers or for students who would like to follow along using the text.

books.title: Introductory Chemistry: A Foundation
books.author: Steven S. Zumdahl & Donald J. DeCoste
books.rationale: Masters at inspiring and engaging students, the authors of this best-selling text combine problem-solving strategies, early coverage of chemical reactions, accessible explanations and visualizations, and an emphasis on everyday applications to facilitate understanding.

books.title: The Chemical Elements Coloring and Activity Book
books.author: Ellen Johnston McHenry
books.rationale: All 118 elements are included in coloring pages, with information about its discovery, examples of molecules it will form, and pictures of how the element might be used in industry, medicine, the military, research, and fine arts.  Also included are a personalizable periodic table and a variety of games.

books.title: Alveary Chemistry Lab Book
books.author: Alveary
books.rationale: This document contains all of the instructions, procedures, and guidance needed to complete the laboratory component of High School Chemistry.

books.title: The Biology Coloring Book
books.author: Robert D. Griffin
books.rationale: In The Biology Coloring Book, students learn about the basic processes of life and the interactions of living things within their environment.

books.title: General Biology, 2nd Edition
books.author: Heather Ayala & Katie Rogstad
books.rationale: In a conversational tone and with reverence, the authors of this general biology text clearly guide students from the molecules of biology to the complexities of the human body and evolution.

books.title: The Fool and the Heretic
books.author: Todd Wood & Darrel Falk
books.rationale: Two scientists with differing perspectives dialogue respectfully about creation and evolution.  This is an excellent choice for students in contexts that perceive faith and science to be antagonistic, as well as those interested in learning to have healthy discussion with those of differing viewpoints.

books.title: The Spirit of the Rainforest
books.author: Dr. Rosa Vasquez Espinoza
books.rationale: A leading scientist explores the synergy between indigenous knowledge and classical science in this courageous and deeply spiritual adventure. This is an excellent choice for students who are secure in the harmony between faith and science and would benefit from a living example in their modern world, as well as those interested in indigenous voices.

books.title: Alveary Biology Lab Book
books.author: Alveary
books.rationale: This document contains all of the instructions, procedures, and guidance needed to complete the laboratory component of High School Biology.

books.title: Schaum's Outlines: College Physics
books.author: Eugene Hecht, PhD
books.rationale: This book is listed as a resource/supplement to the Math with Borders Physics video course.

books.title: Home Study Companion: Physics
books.author: Math Without Borders
books.rationale: This living lecture series teaches the basic concepts of physics by guiding students to mathematically analyze moving objects in the world around them.
